rules_reasoning:
Best-ranked site P002 for Maize scores 61.9% (good), based on matching weather and soil signals to crop-specific ideal and limit ranges. Confidence reflects how many inputs were present (about 82% here).

What looks strong: Relative humidity (51.3%) is within the ideal band (46.91–70.91) for Maize. Clay content (35.6%) is within the ideal band (30–54) for Maize. Frost exposure (0.0% of hours at or below freezing) is within the ideal band (0–0.5) for Maize. Rainfall (373 mm (annualized)) is within the ideal band (150–1400) for Maize. Soil pH (7.40) is within the ideal band (5–8.5) for Maize. Soil organic carbon (25.5 g/kg) is within the ideal band (4–50) for Maize. Wind speed (10.4 km/h) is within the ideal band (0–40) for Maize. (+1 more positives)

What works against the site: Mean temperature is 9.4 °C, outside the viable range (12.31–48) for Maize (ideal 25.31–35.31). This heavily penalizes suitability.

Data limitations: no values for Surface soil moisture, Sand content, Reference evapotranspiration (ET0). Those gaps reduce confidence and apply a scoring penalty.

llm_reasoning: Maize suitability is poor due to critically low mean temperature (9.055°C, far below optimal 20-30°C) and insufficient rainfall (355.145mm vs. 500-800mm needed). These are the strongest limiting factors. Strongest positive factors are zero frost risk and good soil organic carbon (25.462 g/kg). Operational risks include severe yield reduction from cold and drought. Mitigation requires extensive irrigation, cold/drought-tolerant varieties, and potentially soil warming techniques.
